info.title: IoT DC3 REST API
info.description:
IoT DC3 platform REST API documentation.

Covers all service modules: auth (authentication and authorization),
manager (device/driver/point/profile configuration), data (telemetry,
events, commands, notifications), and agentic (AI chat and tools).

Authentication: obtain a salt and token via /api/v3/auth/token/*, then
use the Authorize button to set X-Auth-Tenant, X-Auth-Login, and
X-Auth-Token. X-Auth-Token is a JSON object {"salt":"...","token":"..."}.

license.name: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

tags.name: principal
tags.description: Security principals: manage users and service accounts as identity carriers for authentication, authorization, and access control

tags.name: local_credential
tags.description: Local credential management: create, update, verify, and rotate username/password credentials for user authentication

tags.name: resource
tags.description: Protected resource registry: manage API endpoints, menu items, and other securable artifacts that require permission to access

tags.name: api
tags.description: API endpoint registry: manage metadata for REST API endpoints including path, method, auth requirements, and documentation references

tags.name: role_resource_bind
tags.description: Role-to-resource permission bindings: grant or revoke access to specific API endpoints, menus, and secured artifacts for roles

tags.name: menu
tags.description: Navigation menu hierarchy: manage menu trees, items, and routing configurations that define the platform user interface navigation structure

tags.name: token
tags.description: Token-based authentication: login, logout, token refresh, and session invalidation for user and service account sessions

tags.name: role_principal_bind
tags.description: Role-to-principal bindings: assign roles to users or service accounts, enabling or revoking permission sets for principals

tags.name: identity_audit
tags.description: Identity audit trails: query authentication and authorization event logs for security compliance and forensic analysis

tags.name: tenant_membership
tags.description: Tenant membership bindings: associate users, service accounts, and roles with tenants for access control within tenant scope

tags.name: dictionary_auth
tags.description: Authorization dictionaries: manage lookup entries for identity types, permission categories, and other auth-related metadata classifications

tags.name: mcp
tags.description: MCP OAuth connections: register clients, exchange tokens, and manage authorization flows for AI agent integration via the Model Context Protocol

tags.name: service_account
tags.description: Service account lifecycle: manage machine-to-machine identities including creation, update, credential rotation, and enablement

tags.name: role
tags.description: Role management: create, update, and delete roles that aggregate permissions for assignment to users and service accounts

tags.name: user
tags.description: User account lifecycle: create, update, enable, disable, and manage user identities within tenant scope

tags.name: tenant
tags.description: Tenant registration and configuration: manage tenant lifecycles including creation, update, enablement, and multi-tenant isolation settings

post.description: Modify an existing user's profile (tenant-scoped, verified by ID). Use to change attributes like nickname or enable flag; returns an update-success status.

Page through users for the current tenant with filters from the query body (tenant scope is enforced server-side, not client-supplied). Returns a page of user profiles for browsing or selecting a target user.
Remove a user and their tenant membership for the current tenant (verified by ID). Use to revoke a tenant member's access; returns a delete-success status.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Create a new user under the current tenant and enroll them as an active tenant member. A user authenticates with username and password to access the platform; returns an add-success status.
Generate a random salt for a user under the given tenant, used to salt the password on the subsequent token-generation call. The salt expires in 5 minutes; returns it as a string.
Issue an access token for a user by validating name, salt, password and tenant. Call after generating a salt; the returned token authenticates the user for 12 hours.
Check whether the supplied token for the named user and tenant is still valid. Requires token:get permission; returns validity plus the token's expiry time.

Change a user's password using the current password and a new one, scoped to the given tenant. Use during login when a token cannot be issued because the credential is expired or flagged for a mandatory change.
Acknowledge a client-initiated logout for the current token of the named user under the given tenant. Requires token:delete permission; returns true when the session is cancelled.
Page through the memberships of the caller's tenant, optionally filtered by query. Each row links a principal (user or service account) to the tenant; use to see who belongs to it.
Remove a membership record by ID, verifying it belongs to the caller's tenant first. Returns 404 if the membership is owned by another tenant; logs a DELETE audit entry on success.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Attach a principal (user or service account) to the caller's tenant. The body tenant id is ignored and the membership is pinned to the caller; defaults to ACTIVE status and records an audit entry.
Modify a tenant's editable attributes such as name and enable flag. Non-administrators may update only their own tenant.
Page through tenants matching the query filters (tenant code, name, enable flag). System administrators see all tenants; others see only their own.
Remove a tenant by ID, deleting the isolation boundary and its owned data. Non-administrators may delete only their own tenant.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the tenant to delete. System administrators may delete any tenant; non-administrators may only delete their own tenant.

post.description: Create a new tenant as the multi-tenant isolation boundary that owns its users, devices and data. Restricted to system administrators; returns the new tenant code.
Update a tenant-scoped service account's name, purpose, expiry, owner or credential policy. Tenant and ID are taken from the existing record; any new owner principal must be a tenant member.
Page through service accounts for the current tenant with the supplied query filters. Returns a page of service accounts; use to browse machine principals or select a target account.
Set a service account's enable flag to ENABLE so its credentials can authenticate again. Tenant-scoped and audit-logged as an ENABLE action.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Set a service account's enable flag to DISABLE to block its credentials from authenticating. Tenant-scoped and audit-logged as a DISABLE action.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Delete a service account by ID, scoped to the current tenant. Removes the machine principal so its credentials can no longer authenticate; returns the delete result.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Create a non-human service account principal under the current tenant for API/MCP access. The named owner principal must already be a tenant member; returns the create result.
Page through role-resource binding records for the current tenant. Accepts filter criteria; returns a page of bindings showing which resources each role grants.
Remove a single role-resource binding by its record ID. The binding's role must belong to the current tenant; returns the deletion result.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Bind a single resource (permission) to a role under the current tenant. The role must belong to the tenant; use to attach an individual permission to a role.
Page through role-principal bindings for the current tenant with optional query filters. Returns a page of bindings; use to browse which principals hold which roles.
Remove a role-principal binding by its record ID. Verifies the binding belongs to the current tenant before deleting; use to revoke a role assignment.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Assign a role to a principal (user or service account) within the current tenant. Both the role and the principal must already belong to the tenant; returns an add-success response.
Modify a tenant-owned role's attributes such as name and enable flag. Verifies ownership before applying; returns the update result.
Page through roles for the current tenant with filters such as name and enable flag. Returns a page of roles; use for browsing or selecting a role to bind.
Return the tenant's roles as a hierarchical tree. Use when a nested role grouping is needed for selection or display rather than a flat page.
Remove a role by ID, scoped to the current tenant. Deleting detaches the role from its bound resources and principals; the caller must own the role.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Create a named permission bundle for the current tenant. A role groups resources (permissions) that can later be assigned to principals; returns the new role ID.
Modify an existing resource's definition (e.g. its code or metadata). Restricted to system admins; the change takes effect for every role bound to this resource.
Page through resources (the permission-grantable units) with query filters. Read access is open to all authenticated users; returns a page of resources for browsing or selecting a target.

Return resources as a nested tree reflecting the permission hierarchy (e.g. resource group -> permission code). Read access is open to all authenticated users; use to render permission pickers or inspect parent/child relationships.
Remove a resource permission unit by ID. Restricted to system admins; deleting severs any role bindings that granted it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the resource record to delete; deleting it also severs any role bindings that granted this permission.

post.description: Create a new resource, the permission-grantable unit an endpoint declares (e.g. "device:add"). Restricted to system admins; returns the new resource ID.

Page through principals (users and service accounts) for the current tenant. Accepts a PrincipalQuery body for filtering; returns a page of PrincipalVO entries for browsing or selecting a target identity.
Set a principal's enable flag to ENABLE by ID so the identity can authenticate. Tenant-scoped; records an ENABLE audit-log entry under the acting caller.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Set a principal's enable flag to DISABLE by ID to revoke active access for that identity. Tenant-scoped; records a DISABLE audit-log entry under the acting caller.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: OAuth 2.1 token endpoint: exchange an authorization code (or other supported grant) for access and refresh tokens using a form-encoded body, optionally authenticating the client via the Authorization header.
OAuth 2.1 revocation endpoint: invalidate a previously issued access or refresh token from a form-encoded body, optionally authenticating the client via the Authorization header.
Dynamically register an OAuth client for MCP access from a JSON request body, optionally scoped by the X-Auth-Principal header; returns the client id and a one-time secret.
Update an existing menu node's label, icon, URL, parent or display order. Requires the system admin role; returns the updated menu.
Page through menu nodes with filters such as code, parent and enable flag. Returns a flat page of menus; use for browsing or selecting a target menu.

Return menus as a nested tree and prune it to the nodes the current principal can access. A node is retained only when the principal's granted resources include 'menu:<code>' (or the '*' wildcard); use to render the user's settings sidebar.
Delete a menu node by its ID. Removes the settings-sidebar entry; requires the system admin role for the owning tenant.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Create a new menu node in the settings-sidebar tree. A menu carries i18n titles, an icon and a frontend URL; requires the system admin role. Returns the new menu ID.
Page the MCP tool catalog with optional keyword, risk level and limit filters. Returns tool records exposing each tool's schema; use to browse tools before whitelisting them on a connection.
Rebuild the MCP tool catalog from the registered APIs (dc3_api entries). Returns the number of tools refreshed; call after API registrations change so the catalog stays current.
Replace a connection's tool whitelist with the supplied tool ids, scoped to the owning principal. The previous whitelist is fully overwritten; returns true on success.
Revoke an MCP connection by id, severing its OAuth client from the tool whitelist. Only the principal that owns the connection may revoke it; returns true on success.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the MCP connection to revoke.

post.description: List the MCP connections owned by the current principal. Each connection binds an OAuth client to a tool whitelist; use to review which clients are wired up.
Create an MCP connection linking an OAuth client to an allowed tool set for the current principal. Returns the persisted connection record; the client must already be registered.
Register an OAuth client owned by the current principal for MCP access. Returns the client id and secret; the secret is shown only once at registration time.
List the OAuth clients the current principal owns. Returns client records without secrets; use to pick a client before creating or inspecting a connection.
List MCP tool-call audit entries scoped to the caller's tenant, filterable by principal, tool, status and risk level. Returns append-only records kept for compliance review.

parameters.name: principalId
parameters.description: Filter by owning principal ID.

parameters.name: toolId
parameters.description: Filter by MCP tool ID.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by audit invocation outcome: SUCCESS, DENIED, POLICY_DENIED, ERROR, or UNKNOWN.

parameters.name: riskLevel
parameters.description: Filter by tool risk level: LOW, MEDIUM, or HIGH.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return.

post.description: Modify an existing credential; when the write-only password field is supplied it is re-hashed and overwrites the stored secret. The principal id is preserved from the stored record.
Replace the raw password of a credential by id with a new value. Use to rotate or recover a principal's stored secret; returns true on success.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the local credential to reset. The credential's principal must belong to the current tenant.

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: New raw password to replace the current one. Must meet the system's password policy.

post.description: Page through credentials for the current tenant with query filters such as principal and login name. Returns a page of credential views; use to browse or select a target credential.
Remove a stored credential by id. The credential's principal must belong to the current tenant; revokes that secret for the principal.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Store a secret credential (e.g. password or API key) for a principal under the current tenant. The caller must be a tenant member of the target principal; returns the new credential record id.

List identity and authorization audit entries for the current tenant, with optional filters by principal, action, resource type/id and status, plus a result limit. Admin-only; returns an append-only trail of who changed which identity or permission.

parameters.name: principal_id
parameters.description: Filter by the identity of the principal (user or service account) who performed the action; must belong to the current tenant.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Filter by the audit action type, e.g. LOGIN, LOGOUT, GRANT, REVOKE, PASSWORD_CHANGE.

parameters.name: resource_type
parameters.description: Filter by the type of resource targeted by the action, e.g. USER, ROLE, PERMISSION.

parameters.name: resource_id
parameters.description: Filter by the identifier of the targeted resource; combined with resource_type to narrow results.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by the outcome status of the audit event, e.g. SUCCESS, FAILURE, DENIED.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of audit log entries to return; 0 or absent means no explicit limit.

post.description: Modify an existing registered API endpoint's metadata. Restricted to system admins; returns an update-success result.
Page through registered API endpoints with filters from the query body. Read access is open to all authenticated users; returns a page of API endpoints.
Remove a registered API endpoint by its ID so it no longer appears in the permission tree or tool catalog. Restricted to system admins.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Register a new HTTP API endpoint entry that feeds the permission tree and the MCP tool catalog. Restricted to system admins; returns an add-success result.

get.description: Look up one user by username within the current tenant. Returns a 404 for both not-found and wrong-tenant so name existence is not leaked; use when resolving a login name to a profile.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Username (login name) of the user to look up within the current tenant. Both not-found and wrong-tenant cases return 404 to avoid leaking name existence.

get.description: Fetch one user by ID within the current tenant (ownership checked via principal). Returns the user profile; use when you already hold the numeric ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one tenant by its primary key. Non-administrators are scoped to their own tenant and get a not-found result for any other ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the tenant to retrieve. System administrators may query any tenant; non-administrators may only query their own tenant ID.

get.description: Look up a tenant by its unique tenant code. Non-administrators are scoped to their own tenant and receive a not-found result for any other code.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Unique business code assigned to the tenant at creation time. System administrators may query any code; non-administrators receive not-found for codes outside their own tenant.

get.description: Fetch one service account by ID, scoped to the current tenant. Returns the principal, owner, purpose, expiry and enable flag; use to inspect an account before rotating its credentials.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return the roles within the current tenant that grant a given resource (permission). Use to find which roles can perform a specific action.

parameters.name: resource_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the resource (permission) for which to list the roles that grant it; scoped to the current tenant.

get.description: Return the full set of resources (permissions) granted to a role. The role must belong to the current tenant; use to inspect what a role can do.

parameters.name: role_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the role whose granted resources are to be listed; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Resolve the effective permissions of a principal (user or service account) by aggregating resources from every role assigned to it within the current tenant. The principal must be a member of the tenant; returns the deduplicated resource list.

parameters.name: principal_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the principal (user or service account) whose effective permissions are to be resolved; must be a member of the current tenant.

get.description: Return the human users currently bound to a role (the role must belong to the current tenant). Results are filtered to tenant members; use to see who holds a given role.

parameters.name: role_id
parameters.description: ID of the role whose bound users are to be listed; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return the roles assigned to one principal within the current tenant. Accepts a principal ID (must be a tenant member); use to see what permissions a user or service account has.

parameters.name: principal_id
parameters.description: ID of the principal (user or service account) whose roles are to be listed; must be a member of the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one role of the current tenant by its ID. Returns the role's attributes; use to inspect a role before binding resources or principals.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one resource (the permission-grantable unit) by ID. Read access is open to all authenticated users; use to resolve a permission code before binding it to a role.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the resource record to fetch; use to resolve a permission code before binding it to a role.

get.description: Fetch one principal (user or service account) by ID. A principal is the abstract identity that roles and permissions bind to; use to resolve an identity before binding roles or auditing access.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Publish the JSON Web Key Set (JWKS) so MCP clients and resource servers can verify signatures on issued access and ID tokens.
OAuth 2.1 authorization endpoint: validate the authorization request (including PKCE) and redirect the user agent to the consent flow or callback location.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: OAuth 2.1 authorization request parameters. Required fields: client_id, redirect_uri, response_type (must be 'code'), scope, and code_challenge / code_challenge_method (PKCE). Optional: state (recommended for CSRF protection).

get.description: Fetch one menu node by ID, including its i18n titles, icon, URL and parent. Menu data is global and readable by any authenticated user.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch the OAuth authorization server metadata for the MCP runtime, including issuer, token and registration endpoints. Use to discover how MCP clients should authenticate.
List the tool ids a connection is currently allowed to invoke. Use to inspect a connection's effective whitelist before editing or revoking it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the MCP connection to list tools for.

get.description: Fetch one credential by id. The credential's principal must belong to the current tenant; returns the credential view without exposing the raw secret.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Test whether a login name is free to register. Returns true when no credential currently uses the name; use before creating a new credential to avoid collisions.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Login name to check for availability. Returns true when no existing credential uses this name.

get.description: List tenants as dictionary options (id and display label) for Auth Center selection. Use to populate tenant pickers such as the membership switcher; returns the full option set without paging.
Fetch one registered API endpoint by its ID. Read access is open to all authenticated users; returns the full API endpoint detail.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Publish the OAuth 2.1 authorization server metadata at the well-known discovery endpoint so MCP clients can resolve issuer, token and registration URLs.

RString.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

Content.description: User identity content carrying personal identity details

realName.description: Real (legal) name of the user; free-form text

gender.description: Gender of the user; free-form text such as 'male', 'female' or 'other'

nationality.description: Nationality of the user; ISO country name or code

idNumber.description: Government-issued identification number of the user (e.g. national ID or passport)

UserIdentityExt.description: User identity extension object, embedded inside user VO extension fields

type.description: Type identifier used to parse the JSON extension string into the proper subtype

version.description: Version number used for optimistic locking

remark.description: Description / remark of the extension object

content.description: Extended content payload, distinguished by the type and version fields

UserSocialExt.description: User social extension object, holding social account information embedded inside user VO extension fields

type.description: Type identifier used to parse the JSON extension string into the proper subtype

version.description: Version number used for optimistic locking

remark.description: Description / remark of the extension object

content.description: Extended content, distinguished by Type and Version

id.description: Unique primary key of the record; required on update requests.

remark.description: Free-form remark or description for the record.

creatorId.description: Identifier of the user who created the record; server-populated.

creatorName.description: Display name of the user who created the record; server-populated.

createTime.description: Timestamp when the record was created; server-populated.

operatorId.description: Identifier of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operatorName.description: Display name of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operateTime.description: Timestamp when the record was last updated; server-populated.

principalId.description: ID of the security principal (identity carrier) this user profile extends.

socialExt.description: Social extension information in JSON format

identityExt.description: Identity extension information in JSON format

enableFlag.description: Enable flag: ENABLE (0) or DISABLE (1).

current.description: Current page number, starting from 1; ignored when count is disabled

size.description: Number of records per page; clamped to the configured page-size limit

startTime.description: Inclusive start timestamp (epoch milliseconds) for time-range filtering

endTime.description: Inclusive end timestamp (epoch milliseconds) for time-range filtering

orders.description: Sort order items applied to the query results

page.description: Pagination parameters including page number, page size, sort order, and time range.

tenantId.description: Tenant that owns the queried users; populated server-side from the request context — any client-supplied value is overwritten to prevent cross-tenant access.

principalId.description: Identifier of the login principal associated with the user; used to filter users by their bound authentication account.

nickName.description: Display nickname of the user; supports partial fuzzy match.

userName.description: Login username of the user; supports partial fuzzy match.

phone.description: Mobile phone number registered by the user; used as an optional filter criterion.

email.description: Email address registered by the user; used as an optional filter criterion.

enableFlag.description: Enable/disable status of the user account; omit to return users regardless of status.

RPageUserVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

TokenQuery.description: Query parameters for token validation, login, and password-change operations.

tenant.description: Tenant code that scopes the authentication request; must match an existing tenant. Format: 2–32 alphanumeric or -_#@/.|characters.

name.description: Username of the account being authenticated; must exist within the specified tenant. Format: 2–32 alphanumeric or -_#@/.|characters.

salt.description: Random salt string issued by the server during the login handshake; must be echoed back when submitting credentials.

password.description: Credential submitted for authentication; typically the password hashed with the server-issued salt during the login handshake.

token.description: JWT or session token to be validated or invalidated; used by token-check and logout endpoints.

newPassword.description: Replacement password for the self-service password change flow; {@code password} must carry the current credential in the same request.

RBoolean.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

page.description: Pagination parameters (page number and page size).

tenantId.description: Filter by tenant identifier; restricts results to memberships belonging to the specified tenant.

principalId.description: Filter by principal identifier (user, service account, or system identity) within the tenant.

principalType.description: Filter by principal classification: USER, SERVICE_ACCOUNT, or SYSTEM.

membershipStatus.description: Filter by membership lifecycle status: ACTIVE, SUSPENDED, or INVITED.

JsonExt.description: JSON extension object for generic JSON-typed extension data

type.description: Discriminator of the JSON extension type, e.g. "Json".

content.description: Raw JSON content payload serialized as a string.

version.description: Schema version of the JSON extension data, incremented on incompatible changes.

remark.description: Optional remark or human-readable description of the JSON extension data.

RPageTenantMembershipVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

id.description: Unique primary key of the record; required on update requests.

remark.description: Free-form remark or description for the record.

creatorId.description: Identifier of the user who created the record; server-populated.

creatorName.description: Display name of the user who created the record; server-populated.

createTime.description: Timestamp when the record was created; server-populated.

operatorId.description: Identifier of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operatorName.description: Display name of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operateTime.description: Timestamp when the record was last updated; server-populated.

tenantId.description: Identifier of the tenant this membership belongs to.

principalId.description: Identifier of the principal (user or service account) attached to the tenant.

principalType.description: Classification of the principal: USER, SERVICE_ACCOUNT, or SYSTEM.

membershipStatus.description: Lifecycle status of the membership: ACTIVE, SUSPENDED, or INVITED.

joinedTime.description: Timestamp when the principal joined (was attached to) the tenant.

membershipExt.description: Extended JSON metadata for this membership (e.g. custom roles, invitation context); structure is tenant-defined.

TenantExt.description: Tenant JSON extension object holding extended tenant metadata, embedded inside tenant VO extension fields

type.description: Type identifier used to parse the JSON extension string into the proper subtype

version.description: Version number used for optimistic locking

remark.description: Description / remark of the extension object

content.description: Extended content payload for the tenant; structure is interpreted according to the type and version fields of the extension

id.description: Unique primary key of the record; required on update requests.

remark.description: Free-form remark or description for the record.

creatorId.description: Identifier of the user who created the record; server-populated.

creatorName.description: Display name of the user who created the record; server-populated.

createTime.description: Timestamp when the record was created; server-populated.

operatorId.description: Identifier of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operatorName.description: Display name of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operateTime.description: Timestamp when the record was last updated; server-populated.

tenantExt.description: Tenant extension information, serialized as JSON for custom tenant metadata.

enableFlag.description: Enable flag: ENABLE (0) or DISABLE (1).

page.description: Pagination parameters including page number, page size, sort order, and time range.

tenantName.description: Filter by tenant name. Supports partial matching.

tenantCode.description: Filter by tenant code. Exact match on the stable business identifier.

enableFlag.description: Filter by tenant enable status; omit to return tenants regardless of status.

RPageTenantVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

id.description: Unique primary key of the record; required on update requests.

remark.description: Free-form remark or description for the record.

creatorId.description: Identifier of the user who created the record; server-populated.

creatorName.description: Display name of the user who created the record; server-populated.

createTime.description: Timestamp when the record was created; server-populated.

operatorId.description: Identifier of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operatorName.description: Display name of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operateTime.description: Timestamp when the record was last updated; server-populated.

principalId.description: Identifier of the service account principal

serviceAccountName.description: Human-readable name of the service account

ownerPrincipalId.description: Identifier of the owning user principal

purpose.description: Description of the service account's intended use
purpose.example: Token used by the nightly ETL job

expireTime.description: Optional expiry timestamp; null means the account never expires

lastUsedTime.description: Timestamp of the most recent credential use; null if never used

credentialPolicyExt.description: Structured credential policy (rotation, scope, etc.) for this service account

enableFlag.description: Whether the service account is enabled

page.description: Pagination parameters including page number, page size, time range, and sort order

tenantId.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the query; only service accounts belonging to this tenant are returned.

principalId.description: Identifier of the principal (user or service account) performing the query; used to restrict results to accounts accessible by this principal.

serviceAccountName.description: Partial or full name of the service account to search for; supports fuzzy matching.

ownerPrincipalId.description: Identifier of the owner principal; filters service accounts that belong to the specified owner within the current tenant.

enableFlag.description: Enable/disable status filter; when specified, only service accounts with the matching status are returned.

parentResourceId.description: ID of the parent resource in the hierarchy. Null for top-level resources.

resourceName.description: Resource display name shown in the permission tree.

resourceCode.description: Resource permission code used for access control matching (e.g. device:list).

serviceName.description: Microservice name that exposes this resource (e.g. dc3-center-manager).

resourceTypeFlag.description: Classification of the resource (driver, profile, point, device, data, menu, or API).

resourceScopeFlag.description: Access scope for this resource: ADD, DELETE, UPDATE, LIST, or GET.

entityId.description: ID of the entity (API or menu) this resource secures.

resourceExt.description: Resource extension information, serialized as JSON for custom permission metadata.

enableFlag.description: Enable flag: ENABLE (0) or DISABLE (1).

page.description: Pagination parameters including page number, page size, sort order, and time range.

resourceName.description: Partial or exact name of the resource used for filtering; case-insensitive match.

resourceCode.description: Unique permission code of the resource used for filtering; must match the registered code exactly.

resourceTypeFlag.description: Single resource type filter; ignored when resourceTypeFlags is non-empty.

resourceTypeFlags.description: Multi-value resource type filter; when non-empty takes precedence over resourceTypeFlag. Allowed values: DRIVER, PROFILE, POINT, DEVICE, DATA, MENU, API.

resourceScopeFlag.description: Single resource scope type filter; ignored when resourceScopeFlags is non-empty.

resourceScopeFlags.description: Multi-value resource scope filter; when non-empty takes precedence over resourceScopeFlag. Allowed values: ADD, DELETE, UPDATE, LIST, GET.

parentResourceId.description: ID of the parent resource; filters results to direct children of this resource node.

enableFlag.description: Enable status of the resource; filters to only enabled or only disabled resources.

page.description: Pagination parameters (page number and page size).

principalType.description: Type of principal to filter by; distinguishes human users, service accounts, and system identities.

principalName.description: Unique login name of the principal; supports partial match.

displayName.description: Human-readable display name of the principal; supports partial match.

sourceType.description: Origin of the principal identity; LOCAL for internally managed, EXTERNAL for federated/IdP, SYSTEM for built-in system identities.

enableFlag.description: Enabled/disabled status filter; returns only principals matching this flag.

id.description: Unique primary key of the record; required on update requests.

remark.description: Free-form remark or description for the record.

creatorId.description: Identifier of the user who created the record; server-populated.

principalType.description: Classification of the principal: USER for human accounts, SERVICE_ACCOUNT for machine identities, SYSTEM for internal system identities.

principalName.description: Unique login name or identifier used to authenticate the principal within its tenant.

displayName.description: Human-readable display name shown in the UI for this principal.

sourceType.description: Origin of the principal identity: LOCAL for locally managed accounts, EXTERNAL for federated/SSO identities, SYSTEM for built-in system principals.

enableFlag.description: Whether this principal is currently active and permitted to authenticate; ENABLE means active, DISABLE means deactivated.

lockedFlag.description: Whether this principal is locked out due to security policy (e.g. repeated failed logins); ENABLE means locked, DISABLE means not locked.

lastLoginTime.description: Timestamp of the principal's most recent successful authentication.

principalExt.description: Extended JSON metadata for this principal (e.g. profile attributes, preferences, or provider-specific claims); structure varies by principal type.

scope.description: Space-separated list of OAuth scopes the client may request.

client_name.description: Human-readable name for the OAuth client.

redirect_uris.description: Allowed redirect URIs for the authorization code flow.

tenant_id.description: Tenant the client belongs to; defaults to the caller tenant.

principalId.description: Identifier of the principal (user) this credential belongs to.

loginName.description: Login name used to authenticate; unique within the tenant.

credentialType.description: Type of credential, determines how authentication is performed.

password.description: Hashed credential secret; write-only, never returned in responses.

passwordAlgorithm.description: Hashing algorithm applied to the stored password.

passwordParams.description: Additional algorithm parameters for the password hash (e.g. cost factor, salt); stored as JSON.

passwordUpdatedTime.description: Timestamp when the password was last changed.

passwordExpireTime.description: Timestamp after which the password is considered expired and must be changed.

failedAttempts.description: Number of consecutive failed authentication attempts since the last successful login.

lockedUntil.description: Timestamp until which the credential is locked due to excessive failed attempts; null means not locked.

requirePasswordChange.description: Whether the user must change their password on next login.

enableFlag.description: Whether this credential is active; disabled credentials cannot authenticate.

credentialExt.description: Additional credential metadata or extension attributes stored as JSON.

page.description: Pagination parameters including page number, page size, time range, and sort order

tenantId.description: Tenant identifier used to scope credential queries; all results are restricted to this tenant.

principalId.description: Identifier of the principal (user) whose local credentials are being queried; must belong to the current tenant.

loginName.description: Login name of the credential to filter by; supports exact match.

credentialType.description: Type of local credential to filter by.

enableFlag.description: Enable/disable status filter for local credentials.

RPageLocalCredentialVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

id.description: Unique identifier of this audit log entry.

tenantId.description: Identifier of the tenant this audit log entry belongs to; scopes the record to a single tenant.

principalId.description: Identifier of the principal (user or service account) that performed the audited action; must belong to the same tenant.

principalType.description: Type of the principal that performed the action, e.g. USER or SERVICE_ACCOUNT.

action.description: Audited action performed by the principal, e.g. LOGIN, LOGOUT, or PERMISSION_CHANGE.

resourceType.description: Type of the resource that was acted upon, e.g. DEVICE, DRIVER, or POINT.

resourceId.description: Identifier of the specific resource that was acted upon; must belong to the same tenant.

resourceName.description: Human-readable name of the resource that was acted upon at the time of the audit event.

status.description: Outcome status of the audited action, e.g. SUCCESS or FAILURE.

errorCode.description: Machine-readable error code when the action failed; absent on success.

detailExt.description: Additional structured details about the audit event, serialized as a JSON extension object.

createTime.description: Timestamp when this audit log entry was created.

RListIdentityAuditLogVO.description: Unified API response wrapper. Every REST endpoint returns this envelope; the business payload is carried in 'data'.

code.description: Business response code. 'R200' on success, 'R500' on a generic failure; see SuccessCode and ErrorCode for the full set.

data.description: Business data payload; null for operations that return no body or on failure.

ApiExt.description: JSON extension object for API interface configuration

type.description: Type identifier used to parse the JSON extension string into the proper subtype

version.description: Version number used for optimistic locking

remark.description: Description / remark of the extension object

content.description: Extended content of the API interface; discriminated by Type and Version of the parent extension

id.description: Unique primary key of the record; required on update requests.

remark.description: Free-form remark or description for the record.

creatorId.description: Identifier of the user who created the record; server-populated.

creatorName.description: Display name of the user who created the record; server-populated.

createTime.description: Timestamp when the record was created; server-populated.

operatorId.description: Identifier of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operatorName.description: Display name of the user who last updated the record; server-populated.

operateTime.description: Timestamp when the record was last updated; server-populated.

serviceName.description: Microservice name that owns this API endpoint (e.g. dc3-center-auth).

apiTypeFlag.description: HTTP method type of this API endpoint (REST verb classification).

apiName.description: API endpoint display name. Unique name within a tenant.

apiCode.description: API code. Stable business identifier used for permission matching.

apiGroup.description: API group label for organizing endpoints into logical categories in the UI.

apiExt.description: API extension information, serialized as JSON for custom endpoint metadata.

enableFlag.description: Enable flag: ENABLE (0) or DISABLE (1).

page.description: Pagination parameters including page number, page size, sort order, and time range.

apiTypeFlag.description: HTTP method type of the API endpoint (POST, DELETE, PUT, or GET).

apiName.description: Human-readable display name of the API endpoint.

apiCode.description: Unique identifier for the API endpoint, derived from the URL MD5 hash.

apiGroup.description: Grouping label for the API, typically the simple class name of the owning controller.

enableFlag.description: Enable/disable status of the API; only ENABLE records are accessible at runtime.

type.description: Logical grouping code of the dictionary item, e.g. device_type, point_rw.

label.description: Human-readable display label for the dictionary item.

value.description: Stable machine-readable value paired with the label.

disabled.description: Whether the dictionary item is hidden or unavailable for selection.

expand.description: Whether the dictionary node is expanded by default in the tree view.

children.description: Child dictionary nodes forming the hierarchical tree; empty when this is a leaf item.
